$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to the artisanpack namespace; full attribute schema + supports carried verbatim for round-trip parity.

Upstream builds a client-side preview from @wordpress/core-data post queries; the core-data shim exposes no post entity and this package renders posts server-side, so the fork previews through the package's <ServerSideRender> seam (same as the taxonomy/archive dynamic blocks) and ports the inspector controls to PanelBody (dropping the block-library-internal ToolsPanel dropdown hook). Day-one controls: post content (excerpt/full + length), author/date meta, featured image (+ link), post count, order/orderBy, list/grid layout + columns. Remaining upstream attributes are carried in block.json and surface in a later customization pass.
Dynamic block — returns null; markup produced by LatestPostsBlock::render().
Single v1 entry ported: migrates the legacy string `categories` id to the array-of-objects shape. save returns null.
Upstream has no transforms.js; the fork adds bidirectional core/latest-posts ↔ artisanpack/latest-posts block transforms for the rollout window.
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract; upstream PHP-side init wiring not carried.
Server renderer reimplemented against cms-framework's Post model (a DynamicBlock, mirroring ArchivesBlock/CategoriesBlock) rather than WP_Query. Emits the upstream wp-block-latest-posts markup.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx previews via <ServerSideRender> instead of upstream's client-side core-data query — the core-data shim exposes no post entity and posts are resolved server-side.
Inspector ported to PanelBody instead of upstream's ToolsPanel (drops the block-library-internal useToolsPanelDropdownMenuProps hook).
Server rendering is a DynamicBlock against cms-framework's Post model, not WP_Query/index.php.

A subset of upstream attributes (featuredImageAlign/SizeSlug/Width/Height) is carried in block.json for round-trip parity but not yet wired into controls/render; tracked for a follow-up customization pass.
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that don't exist upstream — required to coexist with core/latest-posts during the V1 rollout.

latest-posts is a dynamic block, so (like core/categories, core/archives, core/tag-cloud, artisanpack/form) it is handled by the Blade DynamicBlockRegistry and is not listed in packages/renderer-parity.json — the React/Vue static renderers do not register it.
